note: These prompts exercise the skill. Running them for real performs live GitHub side effects (branches, PRs, tags) on falconh repos, so execute them only against throwaway repos or in a dry run. They are kept here as triggering + workflow references.

evals.prompt: I just pushed a new plugin to github.com/falconh/talon-aws-cost-report. Onboard it into my talon marketplace so I can install it from both Claude Code and Codex.
evals.expected_output: Inspects the repo; if it lacks .codex-plugin/plugin.json or SKILL.md name, fixes that via a PR + version bump + vX.Y.Z tag; adds matching entries to BOTH talon catalogs (Claude github source, Codex url source) pinned to the tag; opens a talon PR. Never pushes to a default branch.

evals.prompt: I added a second skill to terraform-module-steering. Cut a new release and update talon to point at it.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a minor bump (additive). Lands the change on the plugin repo via PR, bumps version in BOTH manifests, tags vX.Y.0, then opens a talon PR re-pinning both catalog entries' ref (and the Claude entry version) to the new tag. Verifies the tag resolves first.

evals.prompt: What should I name the repo for a new Datadog monitors plugin, and what do I need in it before it can go on talon?
evals.expected_output: Suggests a concise, purpose-reflecting name like datadog-monitors via the naming thought process (no marketplace prefix; repo name == plugin name; kebab-case; domain-named so it survives growth). Notes grouping via a GitHub topic instead of a prefix. Requires both .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json, skills/<skill>/SKILL.md with both name and description, a version, and a vX.Y.Z tag to pin from talon. All changes via PR.
